promptStyle.goal: Give beginners copy-pasteable prompts and ask for one small choice at a time.
promptStyle.surpriseRule: When the learner says surprise me, choose a small option that is not already listed in the suggested prompts and not already built into the game.

quests.title: Make it yours
quests.learnerGoal: Replace the starter placeholders with a real title, pilot name, and welcome text so the unfinished game starts to feel like it belongs to the learner.
agentPlan: Show the learner that the starter page intentionally begins as 'Untitled Meteor Game' with a generic pilot.
Ask what title or hero/pilot name they want.
Recommend a playful default if they are unsure.
After approval, update the visible placeholders and publish the page.
quests.customizationQuestion: What should we call the game or the hero?
quests.recommendation: Use a short fun title like 'Nubsterz Meteor Dash' and keep the hero name simple for the first version.
suggestedPrompts: @G-Claw-Bot call the game Nubsterz Meteor Dash
@G-Claw-Bot name the hero Nubsterz Star Runner
@G-Claw-Bot make it feel silly and arcade-like
quests.completionSignal: The starter placeholders for title, hero/pilot name, or welcome text visibly changed on GitHub Pages.

quests.title: Choose controls
quests.learnerGoal: Choose and build the first movement controls because the starter game intentionally begins with a pilot who cannot move yet.
agentPlan: Explain that the starter game has falling meteors but no working movement controls yet.
Offer arrow keys, WASD, or both.
Recommend arrow keys for beginners because they are easiest to explain and remember.
After approval, add the chosen controls, update the page hint, and publish the page.
quests.customizationQuestion: For movement, do you want arrow keys, WASD, or both?
quests.recommendation: Use arrow keys for the first version.
suggestedPrompts: @G-Claw-Bot support both arrow keys and WASD
quests.completionSignal: The previously-unmoving pilot now supports the chosen movement input and the page tells the learner how to move.

quests.title: Pick the vibe
quests.learnerGoal: Choose a visual mood so the game looks intentionally customized.
agentPlan: Offer a few simple visual directions such as cute, neon arcade, spooky space, or cozy stars.
Ask which vibe sounds fun.
Recommend neon arcade if they want a strong first visual change.
After approval, update colors, emoji, and/or background and publish the page.
quests.customizationQuestion: What vibe should the game have: cute, neon arcade, spooky space, cozy stars, or something else?
quests.recommendation: Use a neon arcade vibe for a clear, exciting first visual change.
quests.completionSignal: The game has a visibly customized look on GitHub Pages.

quests.title: Add a twist
quests.learnerGoal: Add one tiny mechanic so the learner sees the game change beyond text and colors.
agentPlan: Offer one small gameplay twist that is not already in the starter game, not a big redesign.
Ask which twist sounds fun.
Recommend a bonus star because it is easy to understand and satisfying to collect.
After approval, add the twist, explain how to try it, and publish the page.
quests.customizationQuestion: Which twist sounds fun: bonus star, shield, faster second level, or score target?
quests.recommendation: Add a bonus star that gives extra points.
quests.completionSignal: The learner can play the customized game with one extra mechanic.

quests.title: Make your own quest
quests.learnerGoal: Help the learner notice something they want to change and turn that into their first self-directed quest.
agentPlan: Celebrate that the learner has changed text, controls, visuals, and gameplay.
Ask them to look at the game and name one thing they dislike, wish was different, or want to try.
Turn their answer into a tiny scoped quest with a plan and approval prompt.
If they choose a suggested prompt or ask to be surprised, propose one small improvement and ask for approval before editing.
quests.customizationQuestion: Look at the game now: what is one thing you do not like yet, wish was different, or want to try?
quests.recommendation: Pick one small improvement from the learner's reaction; if they are unsure, suggest making the game feel clearer or more exciting.
suggestedPrompts: @G-Claw-Bot I don't like how hard it is
@G-Claw-Bot I want it to look more like space
@G-Claw-Bot I want to make it funnier
@G-Claw-Bot surprise me with one more improvement
quests.completionSignal: The learner has suggested their own change and the agent has either implemented it or turned it into a clear next quest.

completionMessage: Tutorial complete 🎉 You have made a guided game change and created your own quest. You can keep improving this project by telling me what you want to change next, go back to the project-creation channel to start something new, or create a conversation channel from general to explore another idea.
